pr_summary: This PR adds order cancellation and status polling functionality to the order service, including bulk cancellation and discount calculation.
verdict_reason: The PR is given a request_changes verdict due to critical findings related to performance, security, and correctness issues that need to be addressed.

findings.title: N+1 query
findings.description: Inside the loop, a database query is executed for each iteration, leading to a high number of database calls. This can cause performance issues and slow down the application.
findings.suggestion: Use a single database query to fetch all orders, then loop through the results. Alternatively, use a batch update to update the status of multiple orders at once.

findings.line_content: export async function cancelOrder(orderId: string, userId: string): Promise<void> {
findings.title: Missing Auth
findings.description: The function cancelOrder does not verify if the userId matches the order's userId, allowing unauthorized users to cancel orders.
findings.suggestion: Add a check to ensure the userId matches the order's userId, e.g., if (order.userId !== userId) { throw new Error('Unauthorized'); }

findings.title: Potential null-deref crash on order not found
findings.description: If the order is not found, `order` will be null, causing a crash when trying to access `order.status` and `order.cancelledAt`.
findings.suggestion: Add a null check before accessing `order` properties: `if (order) { ... }`

findings.line_content: export async function pollOrderStatus(orderId: string): Promise<string> {
findings.title: Missing Auth
findings.description: The function pollOrderStatus does not verify if the caller is authorized to access the order status, allowing unauthorized users to access order information.
findings.suggestion: Add a check to ensure the caller is authorized to access the order, e.g., const order = await orderRepo.findById(orderId); if (order.userId !== userId) { throw new Error('Unauthorized'); }

findings.title: Potential null-deref crash on order not found
findings.description: If the order is not found, `order` will be null, causing a crash when trying to access `order.status`.
findings.suggestion: Add a null check before accessing `order` properties: `if (order) { ... }`

findings.line_content: export async function bulkCancelOrders(orderIds: string[]): Promise<void> {
findings.title: Missing Auth
findings.description: The function bulkCancelOrders does not verify if the caller is authorized to cancel the orders, allowing unauthorized users to cancel orders.
findings.suggestion: Add a check to ensure the caller is authorized to cancel the orders, e.g., for (const id of orderIds) { const order = await orderRepo.findById(id); if (order.userId !== userId) { throw new Error('Unauthorized'); } }

findings.title: Hardcoded User ID
findings.description: The function bulkCancelOrders uses a hardcoded 'system' userId, which could be a security risk if the 'system' userId is not properly secured.
findings.suggestion: Use a secure and authorized userId instead of 'system', e.g., const authorizedUserId = await getAuthorizedUserId(); await cancelOrder(id, authorizedUserId);

findings.title: Missing test for bulk cancellation of already-cancelled orders
findings.description: If an order is already cancelled, the `cancelOrder` function will not throw an error. This should be tested to ensure the correct behavior.
findings.suggestion: Add a test case with an already-cancelled order, e.g. `it('should not throw an error for already-cancelled order', async () => { await bulkCancelOrders(['already-cancelled-order-id']); });`

findings.line_content: const discounts: any = { SAVE10: 0.1, SAVE20: 0.2, SAVE50: 0.5 };
findings.title: Missing test for invalid discount code
findings.description: If an invalid discount code is passed, the function will return NaN. This should be tested to ensure the correct behavior.
findings.suggestion: Add a test case with an invalid discount code, e.g. `it('should return NaN for invalid discount code', async () => { expect(calculateDiscount(100, 'INVALID')).toBeNaN(); });`

findings.title: Potential type-safety hole: discountCode not checked
findings.description: If `discountCode` is not a valid key in the `discounts` object, `discounts[discountCode]` will be undefined, causing a NaN result.
findings.suggestion: Add a null check and a default value for `discounts[discountCode]`: `return price * (1 - (discounts[discountCode] || 0));`

findings.line_content: const discounts: any = { SAVE10: 0.1, SAVE20: 0.2, SAVE50: 0.5 };
findings.title: Overly broad type for discounts object
findings.description: The 'any' type can lead to type-safety issues if the discounts object is modified or extended in the future.
findings.suggestion: const discounts: { [code: string]: number } = { SAVE10: 0.1, SAVE20: 0.2, SAVE50: 0.5 };

positive_observations: The PR adds useful functionality for order cancellation and status polling
The code is well-structured and easy to follow, with clear function names and comments
missing_tests: Test case for bulk cancellation of already-cancelled orders
Test case for unauthorized access to order status and cancellation
